revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art terrain tile sheet for game development.

Profile: snes16_platformer_v1
Preset: world_grass_variation_16
Asset kind: terrain-tile
Asset type: tile
Canvas and sheet contract:
- Exact image size: 64x64 px.
- Grid: 4 rows x 4 columns.
- Frame size: 16x16 px.
- Total frames: 16.
- No visible grid lines, no tile IDs, no labels, no borders, no text, no watermark, no UI.
- Every frame stays inside its 16x16 cell, same scale, seamless and interchangeable edge-to-edge.
- Background/empty space: solid #FF00FF magenta filling any truly empty pixels only, but the grass tiles should be filled edge-to-edge so there should be little to no empty space.

Subject:
Top-down lush Japanese SASA bamboo-grass / susuki-style meadow terrain tiles for a bright mythical-Japan satoyama world map. Every tile is grassland variation, not close-up village grass and not western prairie. Dense fresh deep-green growth with slender lanceolate leaves, fine sharp pointed narrow blades, crisp pointed tips, upright and leaning clusters. Include broad low-frequency color drift and regional palette variation across the 16 tiles, subtle plains texture, sparse tiny moss patches, sparse tiny flowers, worn patches, chipped/edge-breakup decoration variants, grass-overlap variants, and usable center/edge/corner/inner-corner-like texture variants while still all reading as seamless grass tiles.

Palette and mood:
Bright cheerful 16-bit SNES-style pixel art. Deep rich saturated greens like early-summer rice-field banks, clear blue-green cool accents, tiny vermilion and white accents only for a few sparse flowers, soft warm sunlight. Uniform brightness with ground luminance above 55 percent. No hard shadows. Top-left light source. Crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, fine readable pixel clusters, one-pixel dark outline where useful.

Tilemap quality:
The final sheet must not read as a checkerboard. Avoid repeated square stamps, high-frequency checkerboard texture, and identical grass marks. Vary texture, edge breakup, blade clusters, color patches, and lighting while keeping all tiles cohesive and seamless. Think large geographic grassland/satoyama regions rather than close-up lawn detail.

Strict negative constraints:
Absolutely no round clover leaves, no rounded shamrock or trefoil foliage, no broad rounded leaves, no flat western lawn. Every blade must read as slender and sharply pointed Japanese grass. Not dry, not yellow-brown, not a western prairie, not a wasteland. No Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos, or likenesses. No samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird, no companion creature, no letter emblems.
